mnemonics: All Subtractors Borrow (2's Complement): To subtract, we Add the 2's complement and ignore the Carry-Out.
Half Full, Whole Carry: A Half-Adder has no Carry-In, a Full-Adder does. A chain of Full-Adders makes a Ripple-Carry Adder.
Overflow? Check the Sign! Overflow occurs when two positives make a negative or two negatives make a positive.
keyPoints: An Adder-Subtractor circuit uses a control line (e.g., ADD/SUB) to select between adding or subtracting two binary numbers.
Subtraction is performed by adding the 2's complement of the subtrahend. The 2's complement is created by inverting all bits and adding 1.
A single overflow bit (V) is calculated to indicate when the result of an operation exceeds the representable range for a given number of bits.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the carry-in for the least significant bit (LSB) in a subtraction operation, which must be set to 1 to complete the 2's complement.
Confusing overflow with a simple carry-out. A carry-out from the most significant bit does not always mean overflow; it depends on the operation and the signs of the inputs.
Incorrectly interpreting the final result by not properly considering or ignoring the final carry-out bit according to the rules of 2's complement arithmetic.
